children.heading: Recognition of historic use
children.label: a Recognition of historic use
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure access to National Forest System land by Indians for traditional and cultural purposes, in accordance with subsection (b), in recognition of the historic use by Indians of National Forest System land.

children.heading: Closing land from public access
children.label: b Closing land from public access

children.heading: Authority to close
children.label: 1 Authority to close
children.text: Upon the approval by the Secretary of a request from an Indian tribe, the Secretary may temporarily close from public access specifically identified National Forest System land to protect the privacy of tribal activities for traditional and cultural purposes.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: A closure of National Forest System land under paragraph (1) shall affect the smallest practicable area for the minimum period necessary for activities of the applicable Indian tribe.

children.heading: Consistency
children.label: 3 Consistency
children.text: Access by Indian tribes to National Forest System land under this subsection shall be consistent with the purposes of (commonly known as the American Indian Religious Freedom Act; ).Public Law 95–34142 U.S.C. 1996
